attributes.description: Alerts when CPU credit balance on burstable (T-family) instances drops below a threshold, indicating imminent throttling and application slowdown.
attributes.name: [AWS EC2 OTel] Low CPU credit balance

investigation_guide.blob:
## Low CPU Credit Balance (Burstable Instances)

### Meaning
T-family instances earn CPU credits when idle and spend them to burst above baseline. A depleted balance forces throttling to baseline CPU — applications slow down even when average CPUUtilization looks moderate.

### Triage
1. Check CPUUtilization and CPUCreditUsage trends for the instance.
2. Review CPUSurplusCreditsCharged — overage billing may already be accruing.
3. Consider rightsizing to a non-burstable instance type if sustained bursting is normal.

### Remediation
Reduce burst demand, enable unlimited mode (accepts overage charges), or migrate to a fixed-performance instance family.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/EC2"
    AND MetricName == "CPUCreditBalance"
    AND stat == "Average"
| EVAL ec2_instance = CONCAT(InstanceId, " (", cloud.region, ")")
| STATS min_credit_balance = MIN(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUCreditBalance`) BY ec2_instance
// Credit threshold: tune per instance size — MIN of Average readings is the floor proxy (no Minimum stat collected)
| WHERE min_credit_balance < 10.0
| SORT min_credit_balance ASC
| LIMIT 100
